pattern: Since a number ending in zero always has a digit product of zero and zero is divisible by anything, a qualifying candidate is never more than a handful of steps away, so a simple upward scan from n is both correct and fast.
hints: Start checking from n itself and walk upward one integer at a time.
For each candidate, multiply its decimal digits together to get the digit product, then check whether that product is a multiple of t.
You never have to search far: any multiple of ten has a digit product of zero, and zero is divisible by every positive t, so the scan always terminates quickly.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, n itself already works because its digit product is zero
statement example, the next number up is required
n already satisfies the condition trivially since t is 1
a single-digit n where the digit product is just the number itself
n itself has a zero digit already

stress.description: random n and t pairs across the full small constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 100)
    t = rng.randint(1, 10)
    return [n, t]
